revision.text:
[[ja:磐衝別命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Mizuha no Iratsume|qid=Q108044394|1=Mizuha no Iratsume}}; {{ill|Q108044409|qid=Q108044409|1=Ishikijo-wake-no-Ō}}; {{ill|Q109930199|qid=Q109930199|1=Wakazusuiwake-no-Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Suinin|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}|mother={{ill|Q109931034|qid=Q109931034|1=Kihobe}}|relatives={{ill|Keikō|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}; {{ill|Ojiwake no mikoto|qid=Q11620549|1=Ojiwake no mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Homutsuwake|qid=Q11631423|1=Homutsuwake no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Ikatarashihiko|qid=Q11372186|1=Prince Ikatarashihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Ikohayawake|qid=Q11492324|1=Prince Ikobayawake no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Prince Inishikiirihiko|qid=Q11372194|1=Prince Inishikiirihiko}}; {{ill|Prince Tuburame|qid=Q11394830|1=Prince Tuburame}}; {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}}} '''Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto''' (磐衝別命) (生没年不詳) is an ancient member of the imperial family of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, as transmitted in the {{ill|Kiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kiki (Shinto)|qid=Q842308}} and other traditional records. He is the tenth son of the 11th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin Tennō|1=Emperor Suinin}}, and was born to the daughter of Yamashiro Ōkuni Fuchisoku (山背大国不遅, also rendered as 山代大国之淵) and {{ill|Kamuhatotobe|ja|綺戸辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuhatotobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かむはたとべ, 弟苅羽田刀弁). His full sister is {{ill|Futajiiri-hime|ja|両道入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futajiiri-hime|qq=}} (ふたじいりひめのみこと, also known as 石衝毘売命), the mother of the 14th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}. His children include {{ill|Iwakia Betsuō|ja|磐城別王|lt=Ishikyoubetsu-Ō|lt_ja=石城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakia Betsuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (いわきわけ, also known as 伊波智和希) and {{ill|Mizuhanarō-hime|ja|水歯郎媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanarō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (みずはのいらつめ), who is recorded as the mother of the consort of the 12th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Gohyakuno Ōjo|ja|五百野皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohyakuno Ōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Names == The written form of his name varies among ancient texts. In this article the form “磐衝別命” is uniformly used. * '''Ihatokuhowaki''' (伊波都久和希) – according to an apocryphal text from {{ill|Jōkyūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867356}}『{{ill|Shaku Nihongi|en|Shaku Nihongi|qid=Q2710483|lt=Shaku Nihonki|1=Shaku Nihongi}}』巻13 述義9 第17所収『{{ill|Jōkyūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867356}}』逸文 ([{{NDLDC|991097/354}} {{ill|Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja|国史大系 第7巻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (経済雑誌社, 1897–1901, 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション) panels 354–355). * '''Ishikyoubetsu-Ō''' (石衝別王) – as recorded in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. * '''Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto''' (磐衝別命) – as found in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』右京皇別 羽咋公条 ([{{NDLDC|1879557/77}} {{ill|Gunsho Ruijū Volume 16|ja|群書類従 第十六輯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunsho Ruijū Volume 16|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (経済雑誌社, 1898–1902, 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション) panel 77). * “Iwattsukuwake” (磐撞別命) – as it appears in the “{{ill|Tennō Honki|ja|天皇本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Honki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀|1=Kujiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2716751}}” section of {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}}『{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}}』「{{ill|Tennō Honki|ja|天皇本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Honki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀|1=Kujiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2716751}}」, 垂仁天皇条 ([{{NDLDC|991097/183}} {{ill|Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja|国史大系 第7巻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (経済雑誌社, 1897–1901, 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション) panel 183). * “Ishikyoubetsu no Mikoto” (石衝別命) – as it appears in the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}” section of {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}}『{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}}』「{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}」羽咋国造条 ([{{NDLDC|991097/221}} {{ill|Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja|国史大系 第7巻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (経済雑誌社, 1897–1901, 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション) panel 221).『{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}}』「{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}」加我国造条 ([{{NDLDC|991097/221}} {{ill|Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja|国史大系 第7巻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Taikei Volume 7|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] (経済雑誌社, 1897–1901, 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション) panel 221). == Genealogy == （The genealogy is based primarily on the first appearance in {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, with other texts indicated in parentheses） He is the tenth son of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin Tennō|1=Emperor Suinin}} and the daughter of Yamashiro Ōkuni Fuchisoku, known as {{ill|Kamuhatotobe|ja|綺戸辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuhatotobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かむはたとべ, 弟苅羽田刀弁). His full sister is {{ill|Ryōdō Irimihime no Mikoto|ja|両道入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōdō Irimihime no Mikoto|1=Futajiiri-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65248127}} (ふたじいりひめのみこと, also known as 石衝毘売命), the mother of the 14th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}. His children include {{ill|Iwakia Betsuō|ja|磐城別王|lt=Ishikyoubetsu-Ō|lt_ja=石城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakia Betsuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (いわきわけ, also rendered as 伊波智和希) and {{ill|Mizuhanarō-hime|ja|水歯郎媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanarō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (みずはのいらつめ), who is noted in the {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}} account as the mother of the consort {{ill|Gohyakuno Ōjo|ja|五百野皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohyakuno Ōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to the apocryphal text of {{ill|Jōkyūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867356}} (as included in {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|en|Shaku Nihongi|qid=Q2710483|lt=Shaku Nihonki|1=Shaku Nihongi}}), a genealogy of Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto’s descendants is recorded; his fifth-generation descendant, {{ill|Furihime|ja|振媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furihime|1=Furihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28415396}}, married {{ill|Hikonoshiro Ō|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikonoshiro Ō|1=Hikoushi no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487892}} and bore {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Keitai Tennō|1=Emperor Keitai}} (referred to as Kofutō Taikō Ō, the 26th emperor). The {{ill|Tennō Honki|ja|天皇本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennō Honki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先代旧事本紀|1=Kujiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2716751}} section of {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kyuujihonki|1=Kujiki}} recounts an alternative tradition in which the consort of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin Tennō|1=Emperor Suinin}}, who bore him a son, was the daughter of {{ill|Tanba Dōshū Ō|ja|丹波道主王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Dōshū Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丹波道主命|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368587}}, named {{ill|Matono-hime|ja|真砥野媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matono-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and that he had a half-sibling, {{ill|Sobetsu no Mikoto|ja|祖別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sobetsu no Mikoto|1=Ojiwake no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620549}}. Furthermore, the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}” relates that a fourth-generation descendant, known as Ōyhiko-kun, is recorded.
Relationship Outline Genealogy
: The family tree is arranged from left to right. Solid lines indicate natural offspring; dotted lines indicate marital relations; numbers denote the order of imperial accession. The names are based on {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. {{familytree/start|style=font-size:100%}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||01| 01={{ill|Hiha Suba-hime no Mikoto|ja|日葉酢媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiha Suba-hime no Mikoto|1=Hibasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8375821}} (後皇后)}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||:||||||||,|01| 01=(13) {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|qid=Q316672|lt=Narimutsu Tennō|1=Emperor Seimu}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||}|-|-|-|01|-|^|02| 01=(12) {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Tennō|1=Emperor Keikō}}|02={{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Japanese Hero|1=Yamato Takeru}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||:||||||:||||}|-|-|01| 01=(14) {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Chūai Tennō|1=Emperor Chūai}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||:||||||}|01|:| 01={{ill|Gohyakuno Ōjo|ja|五百野皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gohyakuno Ōjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||-|-|01||||:||||:| 01=(11) {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin Tennō|1=Emperor Suinin}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||:|||||,|01||:| 01={{ill|Mizuhanarō-hime|ja|水歯郎媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanarō-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||}|v|01|^|-|02|:| 01='''Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto'''|02={{ill|Iwakia Betsuō|ja|磐城別王|lt=Ishikyoubetsu-Ō|lt_ja=石城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakia Betsuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|||||:|`|-|-|-|-|-|-|01| 01={{ill|Ryōdō Irimihime no Mikoto|ja|両道入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōdō Irimihime no Mikoto|1=Futajiiri-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65248127}}}} {{familytree|border=0|boxstyle=text-align:left|01|-|02| 01=山背大国不遅|02={{ill|Kamuhatotobe|ja|綺戸辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuhatotobe|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree/end}}
Excerpt from the Genealogy According to the Apocryphal Text {{ill|Jōkyūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867356}}
{{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||01| 01={{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin Tennō|1=Emperor Suinin}} (Ihakumunirihiko Ō
(【Supra|11】 {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin Tennō|1=Emperor Suinin}}))}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|||||01| 01={{ill|Ihatokuhowaki|ja|伊波都久和希|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihatokuhowaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
(磐衝別命)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|||||01| 01={{ill|Iwakia Betsuō|ja|磐城別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakia Betsuō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
(伊波智和希, 磐城別王)}} {{familytree|border=0|01||02| 01={{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Nomutowahakiō
(【Supra|15】 {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōunin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}))|02={{ill|Ihatokishirikake|ja|伊波己里和気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ihatokishirikake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||02| 01={{ill|Wakanome Futamata Ō|ja|稚野毛二派皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakanome Futamata Ō|1=Wakanuke no Futamata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13264213}} (若野毛二俣王)|02={{ill|Mawaka Kasuke|ja|麻和加介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mawaka Kasuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||02| 01={{ill|Itifutafusoku Ō|ja|意富富杼王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itifutafusoku Ō|1=Ohfubutsu no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11493025}} (Dairōshi
(意富富等王))|02={{ill|Agahata Chikun|ja|阿加波智君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agahata Chikun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||02| 01={{ill|Hofei Ō|ja|乎非王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hofei Ō|1=King Hōfei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369801}}|02={{ill|Hobachi Chikun|ja|乎波智君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hobachi Chikun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|y|02| 01='''{{ill|Kanshi Ō|ja|汗斯王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshi Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'
( {{ill|Hikonoshiro Ō|ja|彦主人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikonoshiro Ō|1=Hikoushi no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487892}})|02={{ill|Furibiyamime|ja|布利比弥命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furibiyamime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
( {{ill|Furihime|ja|振媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furihime|1=Furihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28415396}})}} {{familytree|border=0||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|||01| 01={{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Keitai Tennō|1=Emperor Keitai}} (Kofutō Taikō Ō
(【Supra|26】 {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Keitai Tennō|1=Emperor Keitai}}))}} {{familytree/end}}
== Tomb == [[File:Ootsuka (Hakui-nanatsuka).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Hakui Shrine|ja|羽咋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakui Shrine|1=Hakui Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610064}} ({{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hakui, Ishikawa|en|Hakui, Ishikawa|qid=Q822585|lt=Hakui City|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}})]] The tomb is designated at the '''Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto Tomb''' (磐衝別命墓, いわつくわけのみことのはか, {{ウィキ座標|36|53|50.64|N|136|46|46.09|E|region:JP-17|位置|name=磐衝別命墓}}) located within the precincts of {{ill|Hakui Shrine|ja|羽咋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakui Shrine|1=Hakui Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610064}} in {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hakui, Ishikawa|en|Hakui, Ishikawa|qid=Q822585|lt=Hakui City|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}}.宮内省諸陵寮編 [{{NDLDC|1237123/9}} 『陵墓要覧』] (1934, 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション) panel 9. The official form as recognized by the Imperial Household Agency is that of a {{ill|Circular Tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. The site is also known by names such as “Hakui Goryōzan Kofun.” Although his tomb is not mentioned in historical records, in Taishō 6 (1917) it was officially designated by the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household|en|Ministry of the Imperial Household|qid=Q8193428|lt=Imperial Household Ministry|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household}} (now the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) as his official tomb. The kofun is believed to be a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} approximately 100 meters in length, constructed around the mid-5th century.現地説明板。 Adjacent to the tomb is a {{ill|Circular Tomb|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Tomb|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Hakui Ōtanizuka Kofun), which was designated in Taishō 6 (1917) as the tomb of his son, {{ill|Iwakia Betsuō Tomb|ja|磐城別王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakia Betsuō Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579295|1=Q135579295}}. An alternative tradition from the legend of {{ill|Mizuo Shrine|ja|水尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuo Shrine|1=Mio Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548449}} (in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|en|Takashima, Shiga|qid=Q845794|lt=Takashima City|1=Takashima, Shiga}}) states that Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto came to reside in this area to study the Tensei Shintō of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} no Mikoto, and that he died here. Consequently, his son, Iwakia Betsuō, is said to have buried him on Mount Mio. In the wooded hills behind the shrine remain several group kofun (the Haido Kofun Group) from the 6th century, one of which—the Imperial Prince Mound—is traditionally regarded as the tomb of the progenitor of the Mio clan. == Descendants == === Clans === In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto is described as “the progenitor of the Mio clan,” and in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} as “the progenitor of the Hakui and Mio clans,” which are recorded as his descendant clans. * Mio Clan (三尾氏)   A regional powerful clan that held sway from northern Ōmi to the Hokuriku region.『{{ill|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579296|1=Q135579296}}』, 三尾氏項. Their stronghold is estimated to have been in Miou Township of Takashima District, {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} (present-day near the Miou-ri area of {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|en|Takashima, Shiga|qid=Q845794|lt=Takashima City|1=Takashima, Shiga}}, Azumigawa, or in Sakaigun of Echizen Province (present-day near Awara City in {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|qid=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}).   According to the {{ill|Jōkyūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867356}} apocryphal text, the clan was connected with the origin of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Keitai Tennō|1=Emperor Keitai}}, as two of his consorts are said to have come from this lineage. * Hakui Clan (羽咋氏)   A local powerful clan that flourished in the Hakui District of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|qid=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}, in the area of present-day {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} around Hakui City. In the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}, it is recorded under the Right Capital Imperial Edition as “{{ill|Hakui Kō|ja|羽咋公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakui Kō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}” and is mentioned as succeeding the son of Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto. As suggested in the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}”, it is presumed to be the clan of Hakui Kunizō; however, since it does not appear in Nara period documents, its political prominence is believed to have declined early. === National Governors === Regarding the national governors ({{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni-zukuri|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}) associated with Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto, two figures are attested: * {{ill|Kaga Kunizukuri|ja|加我国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga Kunizukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (かがのくにのみやつこ, 賀我国造)   Presumed to have governed the area of Kaga District in {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|qid=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} (present-day the region of {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}’s Kahoku District).『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 217. In the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}”, it is recorded that during the reign of Emperor Yuryaku, the fourth-generation descendant, Ōyhiko-kun, was appointed as the first national governor. * {{ill|Hakui Kunizukuri|ja|羽咋国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakui Kunizukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (はくいのくにのみやつこ)   Presumed to have governed the Hakui District of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|qid=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}} (present-day the area of {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} surrounding Hakui City).『国造制の研究 -史料編・論考編-』 (八木書店, 2013) p. 220. According to the “{{ill|Kuni-zō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-zō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}”, he is recorded as having been appointed as the first national governor by Iwakia Betsuō, the son of Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto. == Related Sites == {{GeoGroup}} [[File:Hakui-jinja haiden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Hakui Shrine|ja|羽咋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakui Shrine|1=Hakui Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610064}} ({{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hakui, Ishikawa|en|Hakui, Ishikawa|qid=Q822585|lt=Hakui City|1=Hakui, Ishikawa}})]] * {{ill|Hakui Shrine|ja|羽咋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakui Shrine|1=Hakui Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610064}} (はくいじんじゃ) – A {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Its principal enshrined deity is Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto. Tradition holds that a descendant known as Hakui-kun enshrined his ancestral deity here. The precincts include the ancient tomb designated as Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto’s tomb. * The annual event held at the shrine, the {{ill|Karadoyama Shinjitsu Sumo|ja|唐戸山神事相撲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karadoyama Shinjitsu Sumo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Hakui City), is said to have originated from the sumo held on his memorial day, September 25, in honor of his benevolent governance. According to local legend, the place name “Hakui” derives from an incident in which he shot down a vicious bird that tormented his subjects, the feathers of which were then consumed by three dogs (alternatively, one theory attributes the name to the legend of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}). It is also recorded that his wife was known as {{ill|Mitarashi-hime|ja|三足比咩命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579297|1=Q135579297}}. * In the vicinity of the shrine, including the tomb of Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto and the tomb of his son {{ill|Iwakia Betsuō Tomb|ja|磐城別王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwakia Betsuō Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579295|1=Q135579295}}, there are seven mounds ({{ill|Hakui Shichizuka|ja|羽咋七塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakui Shichizuka|1=Hakui-nanatsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11610061}}) associated with his legacy. [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) shaden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mizuo Shrine|ja|水尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuo Shrine|1=Mio Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548449}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|en|Takashima, Shiga|qid=Q845794|lt=Takashima City|1=Takashima, Shiga}})]] * {{ill|Mizuo Shrine|ja|水尾神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuo Shrine|1=Mio Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11548449}} (みおじんじゃ) – A {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Its enshrined deities are Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto and the deity of Mitarashi ({{ill|Furihime|ja|振媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furihime|1=Furihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28415396}}). According to its tradition, Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto visited this area to study the Tensei Shintō of {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} no Mikoto and died here; consequently, his son, Iwakia Betsuō, buried him on Mount Mio and established the shrine. The shrine also venerates Mitarashi, who is said to have given birth to {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Keitai Tennō|1=Emperor Keitai}}. * {{ill|Ōminato Shrine|ja|大湊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminato Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579299|1=Ōminato Shrine}} (おおみなとじんじゃ) – A Shikinaisha. Historical records indicate that Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto was once enshrined here. * {{ill|Fukuhi Shrine|ja|布久比神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuhi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (ふくいじんじゃ) – A Shikinaisha which enshrines Iwattsukuwake no Mikoto as “{{ill|Iwa tsuku wake no Mikoto|ja|岩衝別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa tsuku wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579300|1=Q135579300}}.” == Footnotes == '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Original Texts''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''Sources''' {{reflist}} == References == * 「磐衝別命」、「三尾氏」 (『{{ill|Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|ja|日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579296|1=Q135579296}}』, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}, 2010) ISBN 978-4642014588 == See Also == * {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin Tennō|1=Emperor Suinin}} * {{draft categories| [[ja:磐衝別命]] [[ja:%E7%A3%90%E8%A1%9D%E5%88%A5%E5%91%BD]] {{translated page|ja|磐衝別命|version=88003624|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11587884}} [[Category:Male imperial family members before the Kofun period]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hakui]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]]
